figures.caption: Figure 8: The numerical results for Example 4 with loss function (4.17), left shows the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic funtion u(x), right shows the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).
Figure 9: The numerical results for Example 4 with loss function (4.13), left shows the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic funtion u(x), right shows the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).

Figure 2: The numerical results for Example 1 with loss function (4.17), top-left shows the TNN approximation Ψ(y; c,Θ) to U(y), top-right is the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic function u(x), down-left shows the error U(y)−Ψ(y; c,Θ) and down-right is the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).

Figure 13: The numerical results for Example 7 with loss function (4.17), left shows the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic funtion u(x), right shows the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).

Figure 3: The numerical results for Example 1 with loss function (4.13), top-left shows the TNN approximation Ψ(y; c,Θ) to U(y), top-right is the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic function u(x), dow-left shows the error U(y) − Ψ(y; c,Θ) and down-right is the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).
Figure 6: The numerical results for Example 3 with loss function (4.17), left shows the figure of the approximation Φ(P⊤x; Θ) to the quasiperiodic funtion u(x), right shows the error u(x)− Φ(P⊤x).
Figure 7: The numerical results for Example 3 with loss function (4.13), left shows the figure of the approximation Φ(P⊤x; Θ) to the quasiperiodic funtion u(x), right shows the error u(x)− Φ(P⊤x).

Figure 12: The numerical results for Example 6 with loss function (4.13), left shows the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic funtion u(x), right shows the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).

Figure 11: The numerical results for Example 6 with loss function (4.17), left shows the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic funtion u(x), right shows the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).

Figure 1: Architecture of TNN. Blue arrows mean linear transformation (or affine transformation). Each ending node of red arrows is obtained by taking the scalar multiplication of all starting nodes of red arrows that end in this ending node. The final output of TNN is derived from the linear combination of all red nodes.

Figure 4: The numerical results for Example 2 with loss function (4.17), top-left shows the TNN approximation Ψ(y; c,Θ) to U(y), top-right is the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic function u(x), down-left shows the error U(y)−Ψ(y; c,Θ) and down-right is the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).

Figure 10: The numerical results for Example 5 with loss function (4.17), left shows the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic funtion u(x), right shows the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).

Figure 5: The numerical results for Example 2 with loss function (4.13), top-left shows the TNN approximation Ψ(y; c,Θ) to U(y), top-right is the figure of the approximation Φ(x; c,Θ) to the quasiperiodic function u(x), dow-left shows the error U(y) − Ψ(y; c,Θ) and down-right is the error u(x)− Φ(x; c,Θ).
